marketState: neutral, tilting bearish near-term on fading heat and a storage surplus
summary: Front price ($3.209) is 'SLIGHTLY OVER' the $2.929 fair-value estimate now (band $2.429-$3.429), but the model's fair value rises toward the current price over the next 2-3 weeks, so much of today's premium is arguably justified by seasonal timing rather than mispricing. Seasonality shows a modest bullish tilt over 3-4 weeks (56/50/56/63% probability higher, +2.3% typical 4w move) but reliability is rated LOW (51pp spread), so that edge is noisy. Weather is split: near-term (1-7D) demand is running +6.9% above the 5-year norm (hot, supportive) yet fading fast intraday (-19.6 24h), while the 8-16D window cools to -7.7% vs 5y — a bearish medium-term signal. Storage is +175 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish +13 Bcf surprise build, technicals lean bearish on 4 of 6 timeframes (death-cross EMA structure, RSI not yet oversold), and COT shows managed money moderately net short but only at the 31st percentile of its 5y range — not an extreme positioning signal either way. Net read: near-term risk skews lower (fading heat, rich-to-fair pricing, bearish technicals, supply surplus), while the medium-term picture is too mixed and too low-reliability to commit directionally without a better entry.

longTerm.rationale: Seasonality (63% higher in 4wks, strongest month Sep just ahead) and vsMedian strength offer a mild bullish lean, but seasonality reliability is explicitly LOW this week, and that lean is offset by a bearish 8-16D weather outlook (-7.7% vs 5y) and a storage surplus (+175 Bcf vs 5y, bearish surprise build) that argue supply is comfortably loose into peak-injection season.
longTerm.trigger: Wait for either (a) a pullback toward the $2.85-$3.00 fair-value zone for a better-risk LONG entry into Sept seasonal strength and winter contango, or (b) storage surprises turning bullish / 8-16D demand flipping back above 5y normal to confirm the seasonal bull case before committing.

shortTerm.stop: $3.33 (above near-term resistance, back inside 'over fair' territory)

shortTerm.invalidation: A renewed spike in 1-7D or 8-16D demand back above 5y normal, or a bullish storage surprise, would remove the near-term bearish catalysts and invalidate the short.
